name: Richton Bank & Trust Company
description_short: Richton Bank & Trust Company is a community bank in Mississippi offering personal and business banking services with FDIC protection and multiple branch locations.
description_long:
Richton Bank & Trust Company was originally established in 1930 and reorganized in 1932 as a community-focused financial institution in Mississippi. The bank has grown from $8 million in assets in 1971 to $84 million by 2008, maintaining its commitment to local service with 21 full-time employees. Over the past two decades, the institution has expanded significantly, opening a North Branch in November 1997, a full-service New Augusta Branch in February 2001, and acquiring property in Petal to serve the surrounding region. The organization maintains its headquarters in Richton with additional service locations and ATM access throughout the area.

Richton Bank & Trust offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking products including personal checking accounts, interest-bearing checking, savings accounts, CDs, IRAs, and personal loans. For business customers, the bank provides business checking and savings accounts along with business lending solutions. The institution maintains competitive rate offerings and provides online banking enrollment for customer convenience. All deposits are protected by FDIC membership, and the bank operates as an Equal Housing Lender.

As a locally-owned, independent community bank, Richton Bank & Trust distinguishes itself through deep roots in the Mississippi community spanning over 90 years of operation. The bank's leadership includes clearly identified officers with NMLS licensing (Chairman William S. Granberry and President/CEO Jason Welborn), indicating regulatory compliance and professional oversight. The institution has made significant physical investments in its facilities, expanding the main office from 6,000 to 11,500 square feet in 2004 and actively acquiring property for future branch development, demonstrating commitment to long-term local presence.

For consumers seeking traditional banking services in Mississippi, Richton Bank & Trust represents a stable, FDIC-insured option with personal relationship-based service typical of community banks. However, as a smaller regional institution, customers should consider whether the bank's geographic footprint (limited to Mississippi locations) and product range meet their broader financial needs. The bank's website provides basic information but limited detail on specific rates, fees, or advanced digital banking features compared to larger institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: FDIC member bank providing deposit protection up to $250,000 per account
Over 90 years of continuous operation as a locally-owned Mississippi community bank
Multiple service locations including main office, North Branch, New Augusta full-service branch, and standalone ATM
Leadership with NMLS licensing (Chairman and CEO both NMLS-certified) indicating regulatory compliance
Recent facility expansion and property acquisition demonstrating investment in long-term local growth
Equal Housing Lender status reflecting fair lending practices
Online banking enrollment available for convenient account access
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Mississippi, unsuitable for multi-state customers
Website provides minimal detail on specific rates, fees, or account terms
Smaller institution ($84 million in assets as of 2008) with limited product range compared to larger banks
No information visible on website regarding mobile banking app or advanced digital services
Limited transparency regarding current account features, minimum balances, or promotional offerings
best_for: Mississippi residents seeking stable, locally-managed community banking with personal service
Small business owners in the Richton, New Augusta, and Petal areas needing relationship-based lending
Customers prioritizing FDIC-insured deposits with a institution having deep local community roots
Individuals preferring traditional banking over digital-only or large national institutions
diagnosis: Richton Bank & Trust is best for Mississippi-based consumers and small business owners seeking stable, FDIC-insured community banking with personalized service and local decision-making. The main caveat is geographic limitation to Mississippi locations and minimal online transparency regarding rates, fees, and modern digital banking features—requiring direct contact for detailed product information.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Richton Bank & Trust Company is a Mississippi community bank offering personal and business banking with FDIC protection.
